---
title: "WasmPosts"
---

A blog platform powered by **WebAssembly** with **Google Sign-In** authentication and role-based user management.

## Features

- **WebAssembly post engine** — post creation, editing, and deletion run inside a Rust-compiled WASM module for fast, type-safe client-side logic.
- **Google Authentication** — sign in with your Google account via Google Identity Services.
- **Role-based access control** — three roles managed entirely in WASM:
  - **Admin** — full access to posts and user management.
  - **Editor** — create, edit, and delete posts.
  - **Viewer** — read-only access.
- **Persistent storage** — posts and users are stored in the browser's `localStorage`, powered by the WASM module.

## Getting Started

1. **[View Posts](posts.html)** — browse all published posts.
2. **[New Post](editor.html)** — sign in and create a post (requires editor or admin role).
3. **[Manage Users](users.html)** — assign roles (admin only).

### Setup

To enable Google Sign-In, replace `YOUR_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` in `js/ui.js` with your own OAuth 2.0 Client ID from the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials).

The first user to sign in is automatically assigned the **Admin** role.
